title: Calendar & Scheduling
lede: One shared calendar for your whole business — appointments, follow-up calls, site visits, and events, color-coded by category and linked directly to your leads and contacts. Your AI agents can even book into it for you, 24/7.

steps.title: Open the Calendar
steps.content:
1. In the left sidebar, open CRM and click Calendar (or go to /crm/calendar).
  2. The page has three areas:
  • Left column — a blue Create Event button and an Upcoming Events card with your next appointments.
  • Main calendar — a full month grid with every event drawn on its date, in its category color. Click any event to open its details.
  • Categories card (below the calendar) — checkboxes to filter the calendar by category, plus a Settings button to manage your categories.
💡 The little number badge on the Calendar tab in the CRM bar shows your live event count — you always know how busy the schedule is before you even open it.

images.alt: The CRM Calendar page in month view
images.caption: The Calendar page — Create Event and Upcoming Events on the left, the month grid with color-coded events on the right

images.alt: The CRM Calendar page in month view
images.caption: The Calendar page — Create Event and Upcoming Events on the left, the month grid with color-coded events on the right

steps.title: Switch views and move through time
steps.content:
The toolbar above the grid controls what you're looking at:

  1. Use the ← and → circle buttons to jump to the previous or next month (or week/day, depending on the view). The title next to them always tells you which period you're on.
  2. Pick a view on the right:
  • Month — the classic grid; best for planning ahead.
  • Week — an hour-by-hour grid of the current week; best for spotting free slots.
  • Day — a single day, hour by hour.
  • List — a clean agenda of the month's events, grouped by date, with times, category dots, and participants. Perfect for a morning run-through.
  1. Use the All Statuses dropdown to filter events by outcome: No Answer, Rescheduled, Not Interested, Interested, or No Status. Status-colored events make it easy to see at a glance which appointments still need attention.
💡 On a phone, the calendar automatically switches to the list-style agenda so nothing gets squeezed off screen.

images.alt: Calendar toolbar with view switcher and status filter
images.caption: The calendar toolbar — navigation arrows, period title, Month/Week/Day/List switcher, and the status filter

steps.title: Check the Upcoming Events card
steps.content:
The Upcoming Events card in the left column is your "what's next" feed — it always shows the next few events from today onward, soonest first.

  • Each entry shows the event title, the date and time (or an All day tag), the category tag in its color, and the participants.
  • Click an entry to open the full event details.
💡 Make this the first thing you glance at every morning — if a customer appointment is coming up today, it's right here without any clicking around.

images.caption: Upcoming Events — your next appointments with date, time, and category at a glance
Upcoming Events — your next appointments with date, time, and category at a glance

steps.title: Create an event
steps.content:
1. Click the blue Create Event button at the top of the left column.
  2. Fill in the Create Event form:
  • Title (required) — what the event is, e.g. "Kitchen sink estimate — Sarah Mitchell".
  • Description (required) — details, address notes, anything the person attending should know.
  • All Day — tick this for full-day events (trade shows, days off). The time pickers switch to a Start date / End date range so an all-day event can span several days.
  • Select date + Start time / End time — for regular timed appointments. Times snap to 5-minute steps.
  • Category (required) — pick a category like Appointments or Follow-ups. The event automatically takes the category's color on the calendar.
  • Invite participants (required) — search and attach the leads and contacts this event is about (next step explains why this is so useful).
  1. Click Save. The event appears on the calendar instantly, in its category color.
💡 Times are saved in your account's timezone (set in Settings), so a "10:00 AM" appointment stays 10:00 AM for everyone on the team — no timezone surprises.

images.caption: The Create Event form — title, description, date & time (or All Day), category, and participants
The Create Event form — title, description, date & time (or All Day), category, and participants

steps.title: Link events to leads and contacts
steps.content:
This is what makes the iMash calendar a CRM calendar and not just a grid of boxes: every event can be attached to the actual people in your pipeline.

  • In the Invite participants field, start typing a name — matching records appear with a blue Contact or green Lead tag so you always know which kind of record you're attaching. Scroll the dropdown to load more.
  • You can attach several participants to one event (e.g. both spouses for an in-home estimate).
  • It works the other way too: open any lead or contact and create an event from their page — the person is pre-attached automatically, and their profile shows their scheduled events.
💡 Always attach the lead or contact. When the appointment day comes, one click takes you from the event straight to their full record — phone number, history, notes — instead of hunting through the CRM.

steps.title: Organize with color-coded categories
steps.content:
Categories give every event type its own color — appointments in blue, site visits in purple, follow-ups in green — so the month view reads like a heat map of your week.

  1. To filter the calendar, tick one or more category checkboxes in the Categories card below the grid. Only events in the ticked categories stay visible; untick everything to see all events again.
  2. To edit your category list, click Settings in the corner of the Categories card. In the Manage Categories window you can:
  • Rename a category — just edit its text box.
  • Recolor it — click the color swatch and pick from the palette.
  • Delete it — click the red trash icon.
  • Add category — click the dashed button at the bottom, type a name, and pick a color.
  1. Click Save to apply your changes (or Cancel to discard).
⚠️ Category is a required field when creating an event, so set up your category list before the team starts booking. A short list (Appointments, Follow-ups, Site Visits, Internal) beats twenty overlapping ones.

images.caption: The Categories card — tick a category to filter the calendar; Settings opens the manager
The Categories card — tick a category to filter the calendar; Settings opens the manager
Manage Categories — rename, recolor, delete, or add categories
Manage Categories — rename, recolor, delete, or add categories

steps.title: Open, edit, or delete an event
steps.content:
1. Click any event on the calendar (or in Upcoming Events / List view). The Event Details drawer slides in from the right with:
  • Description — the full event notes.
  • Time — date and start–end time, or the date with (All Day).
  • Category — the category tag in its color.
  • Participants — every attached lead and contact, each with a Contact/Lead tag. Click a participant's name (or the eye icon) to open their full record in a side panel — without leaving the calendar.
  • Actions — the Reschedule Event button (next step).
  1. To change anything, click the pencil (Edit) icon in the drawer header — the same form as Create Event opens with everything pre-filled. Make your changes and click Save.
  2. To remove the event, click the red trash (Delete) icon and confirm with Yes, Delete.
⚠️ Deleting an event is permanent — it cannot be undone. If the appointment just moved, use Reschedule Event instead so you keep the history.

images.caption: The Event Details drawer — description, time, category, clickable participants, and the Reschedule action
The Event Details drawer — description, time, category, clickable participants, and the Reschedule action

steps.title: Reschedule an appointment the smart way
steps.content:
Customer can't make it? Don't edit the date and lose the trail — reschedule.

  1. In the Event Details drawer, click Reschedule Event.
  2. The Reschedule Event window shows the Original Event and Original Time for reference, and pre-fills tomorrow at the same time.
  3. Pick the New Date, Start Time, and End Time, then click Reschedule.

iMash then does two things automatically:

  • Creates a fresh copy of the event at the new time — same participants, titled "… (Rescheduled)" with a note of the original date.
  • Marks the original event with a blue Rescheduled status, so your history shows exactly what was planned and what actually moved.
💡 Combine this with the All Statuses filter from earlier: filter by Rescheduled at the end of the week to see how many appointments slipped — a great early-warning metric for your sales process.

images.caption: Reschedule Event — pick the new date and time; iMash copies the event and flags the original as Rescheduled
Reschedule Event — pick the new date and time; iMash copies the event and flags the original as Rescheduled

steps.title: Let AI and automations fill your calendar
steps.content:
Here's where it gets exciting — you don't have to be the only one booking into this calendar:

  • AI agents can book appointments during calls. Give your Smart Agent the prebuilt Calendar tool in the agent editor and choose which Calendar Categories it's allowed to book into. From then on, when a caller says "can someone come by Tuesday afternoon?", the agent schedules the event itself — title, time, category and all — while it's still on the phone. See Agent Tools.
  • Online booking forms feed the calendar too. Publish a public booking page and every submission lands as a calendar entry alongside the new lead and job. See Booking Forms.
  • Automatic SMS appointment reminders. For scheduled jobs, iMash can text the customer or technician before the appointment ("your technician arrives in 60 minutes") and flag overdue ones after — configure these reminder rules in your Dispatch message settings. See Dispatch.

Set these up once, and the calendar keeps itself full while you sleep.

steps.title: For developers: calendar API access
steps.content:
Building your own integration? The two buttons in the top-right of the Calendar page have you covered:

  • API Info (Events) — ready-to-copy endpoint details and examples for reading and creating calendar events programmatically.
  • API Info (Participants) — the same for attaching leads and contacts to events.

Pair them with your API key — see API Keys — and you can sync the iMash calendar with anything.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Questions about the calendar, categories, or AI booking? Email us at support@imash.io — we're happy to help you set up the perfect scheduling flow.

metaDescription: Learn how to use the iMash CRM calendar: create and reschedule events, color-code categories, link appointments to leads and contacts, and let AI agents book for you.
metaKeywords: calendar, scheduling, appointments, events, calendar categories, reschedule, CRM calendar, AI booking, upcoming events
